    • Thank you, Redcar and Cleveland, for electing me to serve as your Labour MP once again. My speech at the count this morning:
    • HUGE NEWS FOR REDCAR AND CLEVELAND: LanzaJet’s Selection of Redcar and Cleveland for Major Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Production Facility
    • New Figures show 2,425 people in Redcar and Cleveland set to gain up to £1,400 a year thanks to Budget Minimum Wage boost
    • WE PROMISED - WE DELIVERED
    • Protecting Jobs at Ensus
    • Great to be at the Saltburn Food Festival. So many incredible stalls and activities,A great day for the whole family.
    • Visit to Material Evolution in South Bank
    • Bridget Phillipson in Saltburn Announcing New investment for SEND places show Labour is delivering change for places like Redcar and Cleveland
    • It’s three years since Russia launched its vile assault on Ukraine.
    • Healthcare where it belongs, in the heart of our community ❤️
    • Labour Stands for Children: Investing in Their Future, Protecting Their Wellbeing
    • Say NO to the Grangetown Incinerator
    • Saks Hair Redcar
    • Supporting Teesside Hospice: My Charity of the Year for 2025
    • It was a pleasure to welcome Stephanie Peacock MP, Minister for Sport, Media and Youth, to Normanby United FC today!
    • TEESSIDE AT THE FOREFRONT OF LABOUR GOVERNMENT INVESTMENT ONCE AGAIN
    • Chocolini's
    • Headland Espresso
    • What a day!
    • GCSE Results Day!
    • Enough with Political Games that Ignore the Victims – It’s Time for Real Action to Protect Our Children.
    • 🌞 Free Breakfast Clubs at Overfields and New Marske Primary!
    • Fantastic to visit Redcar and Cleveland College today following Teesside’s biggest ever single investment by this Labour Government.
    • It's been a busy week back down in Parliament, see what I've been up to below:
    • Anna Turley MP secures a New Investment for Adult Social Care in Redcar & Cleveland
    • Great to meet with some of our incredible local GPs and Practices on Wednesday!
    • ESTON BATHS UPDATE
    • 🚀 Supporting Investment in Redcar and Cleveland
    • Net Zero Teesside Signed, Sealed, and Delivered: Labour Government Delivers on Historic £4 Billion Investment in Teesside
    • Here's a quick update on my past week in Parliament:
    • Redcar and Cleveland MP and Secretary of State Jonathan Reynolds Addresses UK Steel Parliamentary Summit: A Once-in-a-Generation Opportunity for UK Steel
    • Good morning from me and Larry the Cat !
    • Visit SSE this afternoon to discuss the future of renewable energy off our coast.
    • Wes Streeting Visits Ormesby with Local MP Anna Turley
    •  Are vet bills leaving you out of pocket?
    • Lions Sports Bar & Grill Eston in Whale Hill
    • Potholes are a menace, but Labour is fighting back. Labour is going to tackle an additional 7 million potholes this year. So if you see a pothole, report it.
    • Our first 100 days in Government!
    • Local MP Anna Turley and her team secure £50,465 for Redcar and Cleveland residents since July
    • Small Business Saturday!
    • Anna Turley Secures Public Health Funding Boost in Redcar & Cleveland

    • Wes Streeting, Secretary of State for Health and Social care is coming to Redcar and Cleveland this weekend!

    • Britain Remade and Anna Turley Host Public Meeting where Local residents show their support for Net Zero Teesside
    • World Leading Innovation in South Bank and Redcar and Cleveland!
    • Zoes Place - Normanby
    • Anna Turley urges Redcar and Cleveland schools to sign up for free breakfast club rollout
    • You Spoke, We Acted: Real Changes to Disability Benefit Reforms
    • I’m delighted and honoured to announce that the Prime Minister has asked me to serve as a Government Whip in the new Labour administration.
    • ‘Inspiring’ Grangetown Netball Club Celebrated in Parliament by Local MP, Anna Turley
    • Redcar & Cleveland MP Anna Turley welcomes Labour’s plans to bring back the family doctor
    • No more delay - this government is delivering for Teesside.
    • Taking Action on Leukaemia in Redcar & Cleveland
    • Justice First football tournament
    • Casework Win!
    • I had an important meeting with two of our dedicated local pharmacists last week to discuss the serious challenges they are facing. 💊

    • 🇬🇧💪 GOVERNMENT INVESTMENT IN TEESSIDE AIRPORT 💪
    • My Week in Westminster and Redcar – W/C 31st March
    • Tees Valley MPs meet with Secretary of State for Business and Trade Jonny Reynolds to discuss investment in the region after a huge week for Teesside, Darlington and Hartlepool
    • Today, I was officially sworn in as the MP for Redcar and Cleveland - the place I’m proud to call home.
    • Happy UK Parliament Week!

    • It was a privilege to give my first speech in the chamber for over five years yesterday and to do it from the Government dispatch box!

    • It was a privilege to join other MPs and Peers at Buckingham Palace this week to meet the King and Queen again.

    • A Visit to SABIC: Teesside’s Global Impact
    • 🚨 Labour is TRIPLING emergency rough sleeping funding, with £30 million for councils.
    • Exciting News: Redcar and Cleveland will be receiving £1,000,000 as part of the Labour government’s new Start for Life programme.
    • Labour stands united for peace and justice. Yesterday, I sat alongside David Lammy as we addressed the heart-breaking crises in Sudan and the DRC.
    • Its time for my weekly roundup, so see what I have been up to down in Parliament this week.

    • 💚A wonderful visit to Kirkleatham Hall School! 💚

Anna Turley urges Redcar and Cleveland schools to sign up for free breakfast club rollout

  • Anna Turley
  • Dec 10, 2024
  • 3 min read

Labour’s Anna Turley  is urging local schools to sign up to join the 750 school ‘early adopter’ rollout of the government’s new free breakfast club programme - and support local children to start the school day ready to learn. 

 

Families in Redcar and Cleveland will soon be able to access 30 minutes of quality childcare ahead of the start of the school day as the government pushes ahead with free breakfast clubs across the country.   

 

From today, schools in Redcar and Cleveland can apply to become one of 750 early adopters in the government’s free breakfast club roll out, with clubs to launch from April 2025.   

 

Labour’s Anna Turley said, 

 

“Free breakfast clubs are great for parents, great for kids, and great for our economy too. 

 

“Labour’s breakfast clubs will make a huge difference for families in Redcar and Cleveland - they make it easier for busy mums and dads to make the time for work and dropping off the kids, they ensure children don’t start school hungry, and they help families with the cost of living too.   

 

“Labour was elected to change Britain and this is exactly what that means. This is a huge opportunity for local schools to get in early and start reaping the benefits of free breakfast clubs from April next year. 

 

“I strongly encourage local schools to apply.” 

 

Breakfast clubs in every primary school will make a huge difference to children, families, and schools - removing barriers to opportunity by helping children learn, making sure no child starts school hungry, and helping families with the cost of childcare around the school day, to help keep money in people’s pockets. 

 

More than 2 in 5 non-working mothers say that they would prefer to work if they could arrange good quality, convenient, reliable and affordable childcare, and over half of parents say they have problems finding formal childcare for their child that is flexible enough to fit their needs.   

 

The breakfast clubs form part of the government’s mission to break the unfair link between background and opportunity. Delivering change that can be felt by working people, early adopters will offer all children at their school access to a free breakfast and at least 30 minutes of free before school childcare, every day. 

 

This action to kick-start the programme comes fast after the Budget, which tripled investment in breakfast clubs to over £33 million for 2025-26, supporting the early adopter scheme as well as the continuation of the National School Breakfast Club Programme next year, so no child faces a cliff edge on current provision.   

 

Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson said: 

 

“This is a landmark opportunity for schools to be in the vanguard of change, as we build back the foundations of an education system that breaks the link between children’s background, and the opportunities they have in life. 

 

“From helping with flexible working for families, to improving behaviour and attendance, the supportive start to the day that breakfast clubs provide will help drive high and rising standards for every child. This government is delivering change that will make a real difference to families up and down the country.” 

 

Schools that become early adopters will have the unique opportunity to shape the future of the national breakfast club policy, contributing directly to its implementation.   

The wider paid-for wraparound childcare offer – for all primary children to be able to access childcare between 8am-6pm – continues to roll out across the country. 

 

The government is also developing an ambitious strategy to reduce child poverty and give children the best start in life, which will be published in spring. The Taskforce led by the Work and Pensions Secretary and Education Secretary are looking at all available levers across government to drive forward short-term and long-term actions to reduce child poverty. 



Tags:

  • Delivering for Teesside
  • Education
  • Free Breakfast Clubs
  • Schools
